rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type assumptions and guarantees in type annexes; use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts; use valid variables or outputs instead
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by respecting component type and implementation boundaries
Use visible identifiers from the current scope to ensure contract validity
Avoid mixing component type and implementation-level constructs in the same annex block
Ensure all referenced ports and variables are visible in the current scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
For range assumptions, use the pattern `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
For deterministic dependency guarantees, relate visible outputs to visible inputs only
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid placing assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes unless they are implementation-level bindings
Do not mix component type and implementation-level constructs in the same annex block
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
